title: Blood_healthy_control_1
treatment_protocol_ch1: Paxgene RNA tubes were inverted 8–10 times and placed in a -80°C freezer until RNA extraction. Tubes were thawed at room temperature for 24 hours prior to RNA isolation.
extract_protocol_ch1: RNA was extracted per Paxgene Blood RNA Extraction Kit (PreAnalytiX, Qiagen). Globin reduction was not conducted on any sample in this study since it has been shown to have little impact on probe detection when using OvationTM Whole Blood Solution NuGEN Technologies, Inc (San Carlos, CA, USA).
label_protocol_ch1: Biotinylated cDNA targets were prepared according to manufacturer's instructions (OvationTM RNA Amplification System V2, OvationTM WB Reagent and FL−OvationTM cDNA Biotin Module V2). RNA quantity was determined by the Nanodrop and RNA quality was determined by A260/A280 ratio and the presence of 2 distinct ribosomal bands on gel electrophoresis.
hyb_protocol: Samples were hybridized to HG−U133A 2.0 arrays (Affymetrix, Santa Clara, CA), washed, and stained (GeneChip Fluidics Station 450).
scan_protocol: Samples were scanned on an HP Gene Array Scanner 3000 7G according to standard manufacturer’s instructions (Affymetrix).
data_processing: Cell intensity values and probe detection calls were computed using the Affymetrix GeneChip Operating Software (GCOS). Further data processing was performed in the R computing environment (http://www.r-project.org/) version 2.8.0 with BioConductor packages (http://www.bioconductor.org/). Robust Multi-Array Average (RMA) normalization was applied (Irizarry RA et al. 2003). Data were then filtered based on Affymetrix detection call and probeset intensity, so that only probesets that had a present call and intensity value >100 in at least half the arrays of the smaller group (i.e. the healthy controls) were retained. Microarray data were then imported in the Multiexperiment Viewer (MeV) software (Saeed A et al. 2003) version 4.5.1 for Windows XP, and statistical analysis was performed with the SAM (Significance Analysis of Microarrays) (Tusher VG et al. 2001) and Rank Product (RP) modules (Breitling R et al. 2004) to detect significantly differentially expressed genes. SAM test was run using 1000 permutations and a False Discovery Rate (FDR) of 10%. RP was run using 100 permutations and an FDR of 0.005%.

Further data processing was performed in the R computing environment (http://www.r-project.org/) version 2.8.0 with BioConductor packages (http://www.bioconductor.org/). Robust Multi-Array Average (RMA) normalization was applied (Irizarry RA et al. 2003). Data were then filtered based on Affymetrix detection call and probeset intensity, so that only probesets that had a present call and intensity value >100 in at least half the arrays of the smaller group (i.e. the healthy controls) were retained. Microarray data were then imported in the Multiexperiment Viewer (MeV) software (Saeed A et al. 2003) version 4.5.1 for Windows XP, and statistical analysis was performed with the SAM (Significance Analysis of Microarrays) (Tusher VG et al. 2001) and Rank Product (RP) modules (Breitling R et al. 2004) to detect significantly differentially expressed genes. SAM test was run using 1000 permutations and a False Discovery Rate (FDR) of 10%. RP was run using 100 permutations and an FDR of 0.005%.

hyb_protocol: Samples were hybridized to HG−U133A 2.0 arrays (Affymetrix, Santa Clara, CA), washed, and stained (GeneChip Fluidics Station 450).
scan_protocol: Samples were scanned on an HP Gene Array Scanner 3000 7G according to standard manufacturer’s instructions (Affymetrix).
data_processing: Cell intensity values and probe detection calls were computed using the Affymetrix GeneChip Operating Software (GCOS). Further data processing was performed in the R computing environment (http://www.r-project.org/) version 2.8.0 with BioConductor packages (http://www.bioconductor.org/). Robust Multi-Array Average (RMA) normalization was applied (Irizarry RA et al. 2003). Data were then filtered based on Affymetrix detection call and probeset intensity, so that only probesets that had a present call and intensity value >100 in at least half the arrays of the smaller group (i.e. the healthy controls) were retained. Microarray data were then imported in the Multiexperiment Viewer (MeV) software (Saeed A et al. 2003) version 4.5.1 for Windows XP, and statistical analysis was performed with the SAM (Significance Analysis of Microarrays) (Tusher VG et al. 2001) and Rank Product (RP) modules (Breitling R et al. 2004) to detect significantly differentially expressed genes. SAM test was run using 1000 permutations and a False Discovery Rate (FDR) of 10%. RP was run using 100 permutations and an FDR of 0.005%.

Globin reduction was not conducted on any sample in this study since it has been shown to have little impact on probe detection when using OvationTM Whole Blood Solution NuGEN Technologies, Inc (San Carlos, CA, USA).
label_protocol_ch1: Biotinylated cDNA targets were prepared according to manufacturer's instructions (OvationTM RNA Amplification System V2, OvationTM WB Reagent and FL−OvationTM cDNA Biotin Module V2). RNA quantity was determined by the Nanodrop and RNA quality was determined by A260/A280 ratio and the presence of 2 distinct ribosomal bands on gel electrophoresis.
hyb_protocol: Samples were hybridized to HG−U133A 2.0 arrays (Affymetrix, Santa Clara, CA), washed, and stained (GeneChip Fluidics Station 450).
scan_protocol: Samples were scanned on an HP Gene Array Scanner 3000 7G according to standard manufacturer’s instructions (Affymetrix).
data_processing: Cell intensity values and probe detection calls were computed using the Affymetrix GeneChip Operating Software (GCOS). Further data processing was performed in the R computing environment (http://www.r-project.org/) version 2.8.0 with BioConductor packages (http://www.bioconductor.org/). Robust Multi-Array Average (RMA) normalization was applied (Irizarry RA et al. 2003). Data were then filtered based on Affymetrix detection call and probeset intensity, so that only probesets that had a present call and intensity value >100 in at least half the arrays of the smaller group (i.e. the healthy controls) were retained. Microarray data were then imported in the Multiexperiment Viewer (MeV) software (Saeed A et al. 2003) version 4.5.1 for Windows XP, and statistical analysis was performed with the SAM (Significance Analysis of Microarrays) (Tusher VG et al. 2001) and Rank Product (RP) modules (Breitling R et al. 2004) to detect significantly differentially expressed genes. SAM test was run using 1000 permutations and a False Discovery Rate (FDR) of 10%. RP was run using 100 permutations and an FDR of 0.005%.
